title: pandanus terrestris
intro: Pandanus terrestris, also known as the screw pine or pandan, is a tropical plant species native to Australia, New Guinea, and nearby islands. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pandanus terrestris include reducing fever, relieving skin irritations, and providing antioxidant properties. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, and its leaves are also used as a spice in various culinary dishes.
